code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a fluent sentence that summarises all given triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # map predicates to a readable phrase (verb or relation)
    verb_map = {
        "author": "was written by",
        "isbnNumber": "has ISBN",
        "followedBy": "is followed by",
        "language": "is written in",
        "mediaType": "is available as",
        "numberOfPages": "has",
        "precededBy": "was preceded by",
        "country": "originates from",
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "doctoralAdvisor": "was advised by",
        "nationality": "is a citizen of",
        "residence": "resides in",
        "codenCode": "has CODEN",
        "issnNumber": "has ISSN",
        "LCCN_number": "has LCCN number",
        "abbreviation": "is abbreviated as",
        "academicDiscipline": "belongs to the discipline of",
        "publisher": "was published by",
        "firstPublicationYear": "was first published in",
        "editor": "was edited by",
        "impactFactor": "has an impact factor of",
        "oclcNumber": "has OCLC number",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": "has LCC classification",
        "genre": "is of genre",
        "literaryGenre": "is a literary genre",
        "eissnNumber": "has eISSN",
        "frequency": "is released",
        "headquarter": "is headquartered in",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "city": "is located in the city of",
        "president": "has president",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "spokenIn": "is spoken in",
        "influencedBy": "was influenced by",
        "family": "belongs to the family",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "location": "is located in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "notableWork": "is notable for",
        "parentCompany": "is a subsidiary of",
        "regionServed": "serves region",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "demonym": "people are called",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "nickname": "has nickname",
        "genus": "belongs to genus",
        "releaseDate": "was released on",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to allow compact phrasing
    subject_map: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, t.predicate)
        # Special handling for a few predicates
        if t.predicate == "numberOfPages":
            phrase = f"{verb} {t.object} pages"
        else:
            phrase = f"{verb} {t.object}"
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(phrase)

    # Build a clause for each subject, using the first predicate as the main verb and the rest as coordinated phrases
    subject_clauses = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_map.items():
        if not phrases:
            continue
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            # first phrase follows the subject directly, the rest are added as a coordinated list
            first, rest = phrases[0], phrases[1:]
            if len(rest) == 1:
                clause = f"{subj} {first} and {rest[0]}"
            else:
                clause = f"{subj} {first}, " + ", ".join(rest[:-1]) + f", and {rest[-1]}"
        subject_clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single sentence
    if not subject_clauses:
        return ""
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        return subject_clauses[0] + "."
    # Use semicolons to separate different subjects for readability
    return "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: WrittenWork. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
